title: How grill-me is offered
question: How should the companion grill-me skill be surfaced to people who install Decision Explorer?
rationale: Mention grill-me as a separate, optional companion in the README only - don't bundle it, and don't change the skill's behavior text. It pairs well (pressure-test a choice before logging it) but it's an independent skill, so keeping it reference-only avoids maintaining a second package; the note says Decision Explorer works fine on its own so users without grill-me aren't left confused.

tradeoffs.note: nothing to package or keep in sync; just a few lines of docs
a user without grill-me sees a reference to something they don't have, softened by 'works fine on its own'
users learn the pairing exists and can go install grill-me if they want it
both skills install together, so the pairing always works
a second skill to package, version, and keep in sync inside this repo
Decision Explorer stays focused with no outside dependencies mentioned
users never learn grilling before logging is an option
